params.description: View the diff introduced by the latest commit

final_text:
I now have a complete picture. Let me verify the critical crash path.

The change on line 447 replaces `cookiejar_from_dict({})` (which returns a `RequestsCookieJar`, a `cookielib.CookieJar` subclass) with a plain `{}` dict. Let me trace every call site that depends on `self.cookies` being a CookieJar:

**`extract_cookies_to_jar(self.cookies, ...)` at lines 718, 720, 277** — calls `jar.extract_cookies(res, req)` at `cookies.py:137`. A plain `dict` has no `extract_cookies` method → **AttributeError** on every real HTTP response.

**`merge_cookies(prepared_request._cookies, self.cookies)` at line 241** — `self.cookies` is the `cookies` arg; handled as `isinstance(cookies, dict)` so this path survives. But `merge_cookies(RequestsCookieJar(), self.cookies)` at line 478 also survives via the dict branch.

The catastrophic issue: `extract_cookies_to_jar` is called from `send()` on **every request** (line 720), and a dict has no `.extract_cookies()` method.

```json
[
  {
    "title": "Session.cookies changed from CookieJar to plain dict breaks extract_cookies_to_jar in send()",
    "category": "correctness",
    "severity": "critical",
    "confidence": 0.99,
    "file": "requests/sessions.py",
    "start_line": 447,
    "end_line": 447,
    "evidence": [
      "self.cookies = {}  # line 447: now a plain dict instead of RequestsCookieJar",
      "extract_cookies_to_jar(self.cookies, request, r.raw)  # line 720 in send()",
      "extract_cookies_to_jar(self.cookies, resp.request, resp.raw)  # line 718 in send()",
      "jar.extract_cookies(res, req)  # cookies.py:137 — called on self.cookies, dict has no extract_cookies method"
    ],
    "risk": "Every HTTP request that receives a real response (with _original_response set) will raise AttributeError: 'dict' object has no attribute 'extract_cookies'. This breaks all session-based HTTP requests in production — GET, POST, etc. all go through send().",
    "suggested_fix": "Revert the change: replace `self.cookies = {}` with `self.cookies = cookiejar_from_dict({})` to restore a proper RequestsCookieJar instance."
  },
  {
    "title": "Session.cookies as plain dict breaks extract_cookies_to_jar during redirects",
    "category": "correctness",
    "severity": "critical",
    "confidence": 0.99,
    "file": "requests/sessions.py",
    "start_line": 447,
    "end_line": 447,
    "evidence": [
      "self.cookies = {}  # line 447",
      "extract_cookies_to_jar(self.cookies, prepared_request, resp.raw)  # line 277 in resolve_redirects()",
      "jar.extract_cookies(res, req)  # cookies.py:137 — dict has no extract_cookies"
    ],
    "risk": "Any request that follows redirects will crash with AttributeError when trying to persist cookies from redirect responses. Cookie state is lost and redirects fail entirely.",
    "suggested_fix": "Revert the change: replace `self.cookies = {}` with `self.cookies = cookiejar_from_dict({})` to restore a proper RequestsCookieJar instance."
  },
  {
    "title": "Session.cookies as plain dict breaks cookie persistence across requests",
    "category": "reliability",
    "severity": "critical",
    "confidence": 0.97,
    "file": "requests/sessions.py",
    "start_line": 447,
    "end_line": 447,
    "evidence": [
      "self.cookies = {}  # line 447: plain dict",
      "merge_cookies(merge_cookies(RequestsCookieJar(), self.cookies), cookies)  # line 477-478 in prepare_request()",
      "merge_cookies at cookies.py:542 handles dict via cookiejar_from_dict, but only for simple string key/value pairs, not Cookie objects with domain/path/expiry metadata"
    ],
    "risk": "Even if extract_cookies_to_jar were bypassed, cookies stored as a plain dict lose all metadata (domain, path, expiry, secure flag, httponly). This defeats cookie scoping — cookies meant for one domain would be sent to all domains, creating a security issue with credential leakage across domains. The session can no longer properly manage cookie lifecycle.",
    "suggested_fix": "Revert the change: replace `self.cookies = {}` with `self.cookies = cookiejar_from_dict({})` to restore a proper RequestsCookieJar instance."
  }
]
```
